strategy_candidates.title: Step-by-step writing flow
strategy_candidates.short_description: Break down the 'procedure' mentioned in the text into a sequence of user actions and AI responses.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: The text explicitly mentions a 'procedure' that follows the design goals. A walkthrough helps the user visualize the actual interaction described in the method section.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Explain the 'writing flow' as a step-by-step sequence based on the passage and the following paragraph. Start with the user entering a title and describe how the AI assistance triggers and what the user does next.

strategy_candidates.title: See it in action
strategy_candidates.short_description: Provide a side-by-side example of a user writing a diary entry using the 'guided' vs 'unguided' modes.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: The user is struggling with the abstract concepts of 'guided' and 'unguided authoring.' A concrete scenario makes these design choices tangible.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Create a concrete scenario where a user is writing a diary entry about a 'busy day at work.' Show a short snippet of what the 'guided' interaction looks like (e.g., keywords) versus the 'unguided' interaction (e.g., sentence completion).

strategy_candidates.title: The core logic
strategy_candidates.short_description: Strip away the technical terms to explain the basic goal of the system's design.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: Persistent confusion often stems from academic jargon like 'instantiated' and 'authoring capabilities.' Reducing information density can reset the user's understanding.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Explain the core concept of this 'writing flow' without using the words 'instantiated', 'authoring capabilities', or 'procedure'. Focus on the simple goal: how the system decides when to help the user and when to stay out of the way.

strategy_candidates.title: Map the design logic
strategy_candidates.short_description: Visualize the relationship between the researchers' goals and the resulting user interface.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: The sentence links 'design goals' to 'writing flow.' Mapping this relationship helps the user understand the 'why' behind the 'how'.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Create a simple map or table showing the 'Design Goal' (e.g., user control), the 'Feature' (e.g., guided authoring), and the 'User Action' (e.g., adding keywords).

trigger_reason: confusion confidence remained above threshold for 8 seconds while the user had an active selection

strategy_candidates.title: Step-by-Step Writing Flow
strategy_candidates.short_description: A breakdown of the specific sequence of actions a user takes in the DiaryMate system.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: The selected text explicitly mentions a 'procedure' that follows. A step-by-step walkthrough will clarify exactly how the 'writing flow' operates in practice.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Based on the text immediately following the selection, provide a step-by-step walkthrough of the DiaryMate writing procedure. Clearly distinguish between what the user does and what the AI does at each stage.
expected_answer_shape: distinction between user and AI actions

strategy_candidates.title: Guided vs. Unguided Comparison
strategy_candidates.short_description: A direct comparison of the two authoring modes to clarify their functional differences.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: The user's confusion likely stems from the abstract nature of 'guided' vs 'unguided' authoring. Comparing them side-by-side helps solidify the distinction.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Contrast 'User-guided authoring' and 'Unguided authoring' as described in the paper. For each mode, explain the input required from the user and the resulting output from the LLM.

strategy_candidates.title: Design Goal Rationale
strategy_candidates.short_description: An exploration of why the researchers chose this specific dual-mode architecture.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: Since the user is highly engaged, they may benefit from understanding the 'why' behind the design, connecting the 'design goals' to the 'writing flow'.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Explain how the researchers' design goals led to the specific writing flow described. Why was it necessary to include both guided and unguided capabilities to meet their research objectives?
expected_answer_shape: logical mapping of goals to features

strategy_candidates.title: Impact on User Agency
strategy_candidates.short_description: A discussion on how this specific procedure affects the user's writing experience.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: This connects the method to the paper's broader findings about 'over-reliance' and 'authentic voice'.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Discuss the implications of this writing flow on user agency. How does the choice between guided and unguided authoring potentially influence whether a user feels the diary entry is truly 'theirs'?
expected_answer_shape: connection to the paper's findings on over-reliance
